ocr.text: In the second quarter of fiscal year 2025, Barclays reported significant metrics in digital banking adoption under the Digital
Banking Metrics category, focusing on User Adoption and Market Share. The Active Users reached 1,200,000, reflecting a
Year-over-Year Change of 15.5% and a Market Share of 25.3%. Monthly Transactions totaled 8.5 million, accompanied by a
Year-over-Year Change of 30.2%. Total Deposits amounted to EUR3.5 billion, showcasing a 10.1% increase. Loan Applications
were recorded at EUR1.2 billion, with a notable Year-over-Year Change of 20.4%. Digital Revenue achieved EUR450 million,
reflecting an 18.3% increase from the previous year. Customer Satisfaction stood at 82%, with a Year-over-Year Change of
5.0%. The Mobile App Rating was 4.7, which remains unaltered. Fraud Incidents numbered 120, indicating a Year-over-Year
Change of -10.0%. The Churn Rate was reported at 6.5%, categorized as Total. These metrics not only highlight a robust growth
trajectory in digital banking but also reflect Barclays' commitment to enhancing user experience and operational efficiency
within the competitive financial landscape of Europe. The comprehensive data underscores significant advances in user
engagement, financial performance, and customer satisfaction, positioning Barclays favorably in the market as it navigates the
